LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S AMENDED MOTION TO MODIFY ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND FOR LEAVE TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT. V CITY OF SAN DIEGO (1991) 233 CAL APP 3D 699, 707. DEFENDANT'S ORAL MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S MOTION TO NULL AND VOID THE MEMORANDUM OF COST IS TAKEN OFF-CALENDAR BECAUSE A HEARING WAS REQUIRED BY THE COURT. PLAINTIFF DID NOT ATTACH A PROOF OF SERVICE TO THE MOTION. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S MOTION TO NULL AND VOID THE ABSTRACT OF JUDGMENT IS DENIED. AS TO THE PROOF OF SERVICE, MOVING PARTY HAS NOT SERVED JEFFREY PETER WOO, WHO ACCORDING TO THE COURT'S RECORDS IS COUNSEL OF RECORD FOR THE DEFENDANT. EVEN IF DANIEL WONG INDIVIDUALLY IS THE PROPER PERSON TO SERVE, THE ADDRESS AT WHICH HE WAS SERVED DOES NOT MATCH THE ADDRESS ON THE ABSTRACT OF JUDGMENT. FURTHER, MOVING PARTY HAS NOT PROVIDED ANY LEGAL CITATIONS OR ANALYSIS FOR THE RELIEF REQUESTED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION, 301, DEFENDANT DANIEL WONG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ALLEGES ONLY AN ORAL AGREEMENT IN WHICH WANG AGREED TO LEND AND ENG AGREED TO REPAY $80,000. ENG DECLARES THAT HE DID NOT AGREE TO REPAY THE MONEY. THERE IS THEREFORE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. PRINCIPLES OF PAROL EVIDENCE AND INTEGRATION DO NOT APPLY TO ORAL CONTRACTS, AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ARE DEFINED BY THE PLEADINGS. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP SECTION 437(C)G. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR# 8189

calendar.matters:
Ntc Of Mtn To Null And Void The Abstract Of Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S Motion To Null And Void The Abstract Of Judgment is Denied. As to the proof of service, moving party has not served Jeffrey Peter Woo, who according to the Court's records is counsel of record for the defendant. Even if Daniel Wong individually is the proper person to serve, the address at which he was served does not match the address on the abstract of judgment. Further, moving party has not provided any legal citations or analysis for the relief requested. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-24-2008 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
SCOTT T. OKAMOTO, ESQ.(SEDGWICK, DETERT, MORAN & ARNOLD LLP), (415)781-7900, FOR DEFENDANT/MOVING PARTY, DANIEL WONG
MARTIN ENG, IN PROPRIA PERSONA, (415)246-1111, PLAINTIFF/OPPOSING PARTY

THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER THE ARGUMENT, TENTATIVE RULING WAS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT DANIEL WONG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE CROSS-COMPLAINT ALLEGES ONLY AN ORAL AGREEMENT IN WHICH WANG AGREED TO LEND AND ENG AGREED TO REPAY $80,000. ENG DECLARES THAT HE DID NOT AGREE TO REPAY THE MONEY. THERE IS THEREFORE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. PRINCIPLES OF PAROL EVIDENCE AND INTEGRATION DO NOT APPLY TO ORAL CONTRACTS, AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ARE DEFINED BY THE PLEADINGS. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP SECTION 437(c)g. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR# 8189 (301/PJB/LB)

Off Calendar Nov-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES- MARTIN ENG, TEL. NO. 246-1111, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. SCOTT T. OKAMOTO OF SEDGWICK, TEL. NO. 781-7900 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY.

RULING - A R G U E D ; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT DANIEL WONG'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHIFTED THE BURDEN OF PROOF, IN PARTICULAR BY RELIANCE ON THE CONTRACT OF SALE AND THE DISCLOSURE STATEMENT. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT CREATED A TRAIBLE ISSUE OF FACT BY HIS OPPOSITION. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO WONG DECLARATION ARE SUSTAINED AS TO NUMBERS 1, 6, 9, 10, 11 AND 14; OVERRULED AS TO OTHERS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR # 8600 =301/PJM/LB
